steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.crucem: 'Crucem' is a noun meaning 'cross' in the accusative
hints.suam: 'Suam' is a possessive adjective meaning 'his/her own'

hints.to kiss: 'To kiss' is 'basium dare' or 'osculari' — 'baiulare' means 'to carry'

hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'baiulavit' — 'baiulat' has the present stem
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'baiulet' — 'baiulat' ends in -at (1st conj. indicative)

hints.fero, ferre, tuli, latum: These are the parts of 'ferre' (to carry) — a synonym with irregular parts
hints.porto, portare, portavi, portatum: These are the parts of 'portare' (to carry) — a synonym but different verb
hints.gero, gerere, gessi, gestum: These are the parts of 'gerere' (to carry/wage)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'crux' — the -em ending marks accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'cruce' — 'crucem' with -em is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'crucis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'crucis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'crucis' ends in -is

hints.nominative feminine singular: Nominative feminine would be 'sua' — the -am ending marks accusative
hints.ablative feminine singular: Ablative feminine would be 'sua' — 'suam' with -am is accusative
hints.accusative masculine singular: Accusative masculine would be 'suum' — 'suam' agrees with 'crucem' (feminine)

steps.prompt: What type of clause does 'Qui' introduce here?

hints.indirect question: An indirect question would require subjunctive — 'baiulat' is indicative
hints.purpose clause: A purpose clause uses 'ut/ne' + subjunctive, not 'qui' + indicative
hints.causal clause: A causal 'qui' would require subjunctive — 'baiulat' is indicative

translation: Who does not carry his own cross
